title: Apache Security Advisories - 2022-01-18
description:
Merged 3 apache security advisories published on 2022-01-18. Original advisory IDs: CVE-2022-23302, CVE-2022-23305, CVE-2022-23307

Titles:
- Apache Kafka: Deserialization of Untrusted Data Flaw in JMSSink of Apache Log4j logging library in versions 1.x
- Apache Kafka: SQL injection Flaw in Apache Log4j logging library in versions 1.x
- Apache Kafka: Deserialization of Untrusted Data Flaw in Apache Log4j logging library in versions 1.x
fixed_versions: In the absence of a new log4j 1.x release
one can remove JMSSink class from the log4j-1.2.17.jar artifact.
one can remove JDBCAppender class from the log4j-1.2.17.jar artifact.
one can remove Chainsaw from the log4j-1.2.17.jar artifact.

cves.title: Deserialization of Untrusted Data Flaw in JMSSink of Apache Log4j logging library in versions 1.x
SQL injection Flaw in Apache Log4j logging library in versions 1.x
Deserialization of Untrusted Data Flaw in Apache Log4j logging library in versions 1.x
